transcript: Hey friends, it's Tracy. Welcome to another collection video of my Easter and Spring DIYs. Today I'm sharing some floral ideas that I have here on my channel and I'm so glad that you're here. Without further ado, let's get started. So hearts are not just for Valentine's. You can definitely use them anytime throughout the year, especially the springtime now what I'm starting with here are my supplies that I got from you know different craft stores so as I go throughout the video I will share where I got those particular craft supplies now what I'm starting with is one of these wood hearts and these are in the crafter square section of the dollar tree if you do not or are not fortunate enough to find these you can use maybe some leftover hearts that you have from the valentine section or you can definitely cut it out of foam board or cardboard or something that's a little bit stiffer because you definitely want to have some form to your project so let's go ahead and get into the project and I'm going to share what I did to create this little spring DIY that you can definitely use a valentine leftover crafts okay I'm going to paint my heart with two coats of white chalk paint and I just go around the edges and then I paint the front of the heart with two coats of the white chalk paint. Then to give a bit of a rustic farmhouse look that I'm going for, I'm using this acrylic paint. This is the color burlap and I have a stencil brush and I just put some out on my little paint plate and I just am dabbing it around the heart just kind of stippling it and just blending it in as I go around and then I will go back over the heart just in different places kind of like where it would be kind of like a board or shiplap kind of look and then I have a fine sharpie marker just going to go around the edges just to give it a bit of doodling because I like that look and then I have some black paint and a stiff stencil brush i I'm going to splatter this heart with some black paint because I like the splattering look. You can totally cut out any of these steps that you do not like. And then to make the little pocket, I'm taking my burlap. I don't like the edges, you know, those seams. So I'm just hot gluing those down and then I will make a little pocket for my heart. Thank you. i'm going to give a little tip that i have found works nicely i want to put two holes in my heart so that my wire can go through there and i can put pull out my electric drill but I wanted to share this tip with you because these hearts are very soft wood if you use your poking tool it will and you know just a little elbow grease a little pressure it pokes through this wood quite nicely So if you don't have, you a drill or don't want to pull it out you can maybe try to see if you can get a little hole in there because you know I know some people don't have a lot of different electric tools and sometimes we're looking for different ways to use different little gadgets I will have a link in my description below for the poking tool that I use. You can see exactly what it looks like to look in the craft store or you can check Amazon to have it shipped directly to you. So then now I'm using my silver wire and I like to use the wire from the Dollar Tree. Now I've had the silver wire for a while the wire i get is in the automotive section at dollar tree and it used to come with silver red and black now it seems to just be coming with red and black but this is the wire that i use and it is soft and it's pliable it's bendable and I really like you know the the grade that it is and so I just am putting that through there and then I'm going to string I'm using 10 wood beads I'm going to do five on one side and five on the other and so I just string those on there and then I just kind of bend it bend that wire the way that i want my beads to show now the fun part begins for me because I just take these little flowers and start filling up my pockets. I have some cherry blossoms from the Dollar Tree as well as some of these pink wildflowers. The greenery is from Walmart. It is the boxwood and then this these white berries kind of look like baby's breath to me and those are also from Walmart on a little bundle. And then I just glue everything into the pocket until I like the look I'm going, you know, putting it to one side because on the other side, that's where I'm going to adhere my heart. These pit berry little sections, uh, is from Hobby Lobby. I actually purchased a garland and they were individual little pit berry sections that were tied on the garland. And so I just took the garland apart. They're carried all the time in Hobby Lobby and they're in the garland section back in the floral section. So if you want to look for those I love pit berries I love any kind of berries and so these really speak to my heart and I Dollar Tree that they did have during the valentine season now i just want to encourage anyone that's watching this later think outside the box you can get hearts anywhere and they're not just for valentine's day so you may find hearts anytime throughout the year just because i'm using this particular one doesn't mean that you need to make it exactly the way that I do these are just ideas that I like to share so I encourage you to think outside of the box and use what you may already have so I just painted the heart with some royal fuchsia americana paint and I just sanded the edges then now what I'm doing I have this thick fun foam that I have in my craft room that I used for projects like this and it gives some height to my project so I'm just cutting off a piece of that and then I glue it onto the wood heart and then I have the smaller heart that I glue on top and it gives a bit of dimension to my project. I like to use this jewelry cording or hemp cording that you can get in the jewelry section. You can get it at any of the craft stores, Walmart has it, even I've seen it at Dollar Tree, I've've seen it at dollar general anyway i'm just making six loops which is three loops on each side and then i just tear off a piece of that and then tie it together just make a little knot and then i'm going to cover up that hole that's in the wood heart and then i'm going to put on a cute little button. supplies i'm using for this arrangement the bunny butt came from dollar tree the easter pick came from hobby lobby the spanish moss came from walmart now to fill my uh bucket i am going to use this tight foam and okay guys here's a little update since I have done this video I have since discovered this gaps and crack filler I pick it up at my local grocery store it is in the automotive section so I am chalking up to a bad can when I previously did this little floral arrangement because the gaps and cracks did not come out of that can that tight foam the way that it should. So I like to use this. I like to fill my different containers up with it because it's if I don't have any styrofoam or something like that. A lot of floral designers, a lot of floral designers a lot of wreath makers creators that do floral design use the gaps and crack it just kind of helps you can fill up different containers kind of let it dry i let it dry for several hours or either overnight and it just works just like styrofoam just to stick your picks in your greenery your floral that kind of thing it's just another option uh if you don't have styrofoam or if you don't want to use styrofoam that kind of thing and depending on how much you use will depend on how many containers you can fill up and so i just love using it but that's a little update you. Different textures and greenery, some pit berries, just different things that I had in my stash that I wanted to add to my arrangement. For my bow, I'm using this burlap polka dot ribbon. Now this came from Sam's and I'm just using, or I'm doing a six loop bow, three on each side. And so what is i make a loop twist it in the middle then i turn it like flip it so that the right side of the ribbon is on top and i'll just continue doing that by twisting it in the middle and flipping it so the right side of the ribbon is on top now i was uh to secure it all i used a zip tie and then i was going to use a you pin to stick it into the foam but it didn't work it's too stiff so I had to pull out one of these floral picks to get it to stick into that foam again I'm not a fan okay so one of these signs I I do have these signs in my Amazon but uh they're a little bit longer and so i had to kind of cut it down i just used my box blade and my scissors just to kind of give it the jagged edge on one of the ends and so the happy easter is uh from a pack from hobby lobby and i just painted that with poodle skirt pink then for the sign I just use some plaster chalk paint and my just doing my faux plaid pattern I'm doing that with drizzle gray then I kind of work in sections as I'm you know painting little signs like this you know like one while one's drying I work on a different part of it so I added some dots just some polka dots with my stylus to the Happy Easter sign and so then while that's drying then I am adding some brown shading to my sign alright so I have one of these large craft sticks I get them from a pack from Walmart and then just cut a point at the end. I added some stain to it with a baby wipe and then Once I get that all stained then I will add just a little bit of dry brushing stick my sign in there just like that and this is how it looks all together I just love it so much just those little bunny butts just really add a lot to your spring arrangement I'm preparing my tin bucket and using some Aleene's tacky glue i just glue the styrofoam in the bucket and so as i was looking at this i was thinking this may be a little bit small so the next time i went to hobby lobby i picked up another one of their medium buckets which i like a little bit better so it's just a personal preference different choice so I just filled the medium bucket with some styrofoam because that will give me a little bit more room and space to include all of my cute things that I want to include in this bucket to paint my wood bunny I got that wood bunny over at the dollar general i'm using a one of the wooden plant labels from the 99 cent store that is going to be what i'm going to adhere the bunny to and attach it into the styrofoam so now that i have my bunny all painted i did two coats of the paint on the bunny and so now I just have my little sanding tool and I'm just going to rough give a little bit of distressing have some of that natural wood come through so I'm just taking my little sanding tool and I'm just going to go all the way around exposing some of that little natural wood then I take my cheesecloth or my tack cloth I will have a link below because I do get questions about that I love this using for little woodworking items like this it gets off all the little dust particles and especially if I'm you know working with glitter anything that has the little dust really helps me, you know, keep my area clean as well as get all of that off of my projects. I prefer to seal my wooden items and my favorite varnish is to use is this Delta Ceramic Coat Glossy Varnish. So I just put a layer of that on top of all of my wooden wooden items I will have a link to that in the description below of exactly which one I use to attach the plant label to the back of my bunny the plant label is just a tad long so I cut it off with my scissors and then I'm going to use a combination of hot glue and this rapid fuse glue the wood version the the rapid fuse glue dries pretty quick but to give in an instant hole I'm using the combination of that as well as hot glue so now I'm just gonna figure out how or where I want my bunny in this little can and so once I figure out the placement I just hot glue that in the can and then now I will work around the bunny putting all of my little flowers and berries and other i thought that it would give it a pop of color as well as break up a little bit of that other color of excelsior so now i'm just looking at this and i've discovered that the handle from the tin bucket was behind my bunny and i didn't like that so i'm like what am i going to do so to my delight my bunny was not all the way glued in the styrofoam so I just pulled it out and then you know turned my handle to the front or actually brought it to the front and then now I'm just going to hot glue my bunny back into it so no problem I just wanted that handle in the front. For the greenery it's a little bit short so i use my steel picks machine to put a floral pick on the bottom of each of the little greenery so that i could stick it into the styrofoam now i got my steel picks machine from hobby lobby it is not definitely anything that you need to have in order to create a project like this. It's just a gadget that I have in my craft room, so I'm using it. But anyway, it just provides a little bit of length on the bottom of florals so that, you know, you can insert it into styrofoam. The cherry blossoms are from the Dollar Tree. I got this color of pink as well as a white one I just love cherry blossoms I think that they're just so pretty I just popped off a few of the pink cherry blossoms from the stem just hot glue in them randomly sporadically throughout the little grass and I've decided to do that to have a little dimension with my flowers because then for the white ones and the other cherry blossoms I'm just going to cut them off the stem put one of those steel picks on there so want to figure out where I want to put my eggs and I have three of these little plastic speckled eggs and it already has a little hole in the bottom of this egg so I'm just kind of using that hole sticking a toothpick through there and then I'm just going to hot glue the eggs into the styrofoam placing them where I think they look really cute. cute baby's breath is just what this little arrangement needs for me this is also from the tree it called Gypso. So I just cut off a few sprigs of those and just include it in the little arrangement. I love the wispiness, if that's even a word um i just love the wispiness of this little arrangement i love the way that that gypso just really adds that extra touch to this whole little arrangement the cherry blossoms the greenery the bunny i'm really glad that i decided to go with the medium tin bucket from Hobby Lobby instead of just trying to cram all of this stuff into that smaller one that arrangement okay so these are the supplies i'm using the flowers came from walmart i'm using one of those pales from dollar tree and then some styrofoam and some pit berries now these pit berries came that I love for my country rustic projects just adds a lot of softness and so you know I just kind of just continue to add into my little floral arrangement and what I was showing a while ago with the pit berries that have the steel pick that I use my steel picks machine and it really helps with securing my florals in place in the styrofoam and this is just the perfect little floral arrangement to add a bit of pink and spring into my craft room. okay for this cute project is a bunny hop decor piece that i created using one of these wood pieces from the dollar tree and i'm also going to be using the Mod Podge heat transfer method and first I put a coat of Mod Podge on the wood bunny. I paid special attention to the edges just to make sure that they were all nice and covered so that I wouldn't have any of my paper you know come up. So I want to capture a specific part of the paper so I just take my pen something that I can see and then just carefully draw around the bunny and then I will cut it out with scissors and then that is what I'm going to Mod Podge on to my wooden bunny. If you are not familiar with the mod podge heat transfer method what it is is i put a layer of mod podge on my wood bunny and it completely dried i had my heat gun or my heat tool to help it along so that it's completely dry and then i'm using my mini heat press that i have that i bought from Cricut and I'm just using that as an iron but a regular iron or other mini iron may work and just using some parchment paper and I make sure that my paper is on the you know bunny secured and then I you know heat it up and pay special attention to the edges and since I've been doing this I do not have any paper peel up. For the banner of in front of my basket what I started with is this piece of burlap. Now this burlap is just a strip that I've had left over from a previous project and it already has mod Podge on it. I like to put Mod Podge on my burlap to make it a bit stiffer. And so then I just took just some cardstock and just made me a template for a small banner, little pennant. And then I'm going to trace this out. I found that a fine Sharpie marker shows up best for me so I traced this out and then just cut it out so that it'll be ready I'm going to paint just a part of the banner just to give it some pink color and it's gonna look really cute on the front of my basket now for the letters what i'm using is these hop or i just got these letters from hobby lobby and i'm just using this uh purple color it's uh the color lavender and i just gave that one coat of lavender color paint and then i'm going to go back and add just some white highlight to the letters because that's what I like to do just to make them pop for the banner I'm using this pink color it is an Americana brand poodle skirt pink is the name of this pink so I just took my quarter inch flat paintbrush and just went around kind of in the center just to give it some pink color I am NOT going all the way to the end as you can see I'm just going around just to kind of fill in the middle of that banner and then I will just glue the letters on to the front of the banner these small baskets came from Hobby Lobby during the fall season and I love them because it looks like little bushel baskets to me and what I did I didn't do anything to the basket this is this I left it the color that it is and so then I'm just going to glue these pennants to make the banner to spell hop on the front of the basket. to finish off the edges of the banner i just made a two loop bow just with some jute that i got from the dollar tree and then i just glue that to the ends of each of the banner just to look like it's all tied together to decorate my basket i'm just using a block of this styrofoam that I like to get from the Dollar Tree when I can find it I just glue that in and then to cover it up I'm just using this fine excelsior I love this fine excelsior it's kind of like Spanish moss and I have the best luck finding it at Joann's craft store and what I do is just cover up that styrofoam and just push it into the basket just trying to you know bring it down so that I don't see any of the green of that styrofoam and then this is what I'm going to stick my florals in now this greenery with the small purple flowers came from Hobby Lobby of course and it just says floral on it. There's no you know specific name or anything. So I just cut off sticks of this or sprigs of this rather. And this particular greenery comes with the little white flowers. These purple flowers, yellow and I think pink. And I cannot wait to get some of those other colors as well and so then I just continue just to put these little sprigs of this greenery with the purple flower in until I like the look of it and then I'm going to attach the basket to my bunny but I want to make sure that my bunny stands up and that you know everything is you know proportioned so that he doesn't lean one way or the other so i just hot glue that basket to the front of the bunny um actually i used a combination of e6000 and a bit of the hot glue just to make sure i got a permanent hold and then those white baby's breath i got those from Walmart and then I cut off a few sprigs of those and stuck that in there and it was perfect for this little bunny for the bow at the top of her ear what I did is I just cut off some strips of just burlap and I have this purple small dot polka dot ribbon that I got from Hobby Lobby I love that so much it's perfect for the spring and Easter season and I also have some just different ribbons and lace this one is glittery and then I have some lace that I got also from Hobby Lobby probably most of these came from Hobby Lobby or either Walmart I think that white and pink pom-pom ribbon i got from walmart and so i just crisscross those until i like the look and then i get the best hold with a pipe cleaner so i just put a pipe cleaner around it and i'm not too worried about it you know that you're going to see the pipe cleaner because i'm going to um you know glue a cute flower to the center of this messy bow. These flowers came from nowhere else but Hobby Lobby in the Easter section and so I just went through and just picked out one that I liked and just glued it there in the center of that bow and I think it is the perfect addition to this bunny hop decor piece that I created. We'll see you next time. Thank you.
